reviewsSummary.note: Round 3 re-run: reviewed 3 new fix features (fix-execution-results-and-cancel, fix-responses-vbr-iloc, fix-pagination-controls) completed after round 2. All passed. Cumulative across all rounds: 9 features reviewed, 9 passed.

appliedUpdates.description: Added 'WebSocket length=10000 and VBR amplification' note to the WebSocket Protocol section: documents why length=10000 is passed to QueryResults in send_query_update() and warns about ~435MB payloads for VBR queries at that limit.

Added 'Stale Closure Pattern in ExecutionContext.tsx' section documenting the mirror-state-in-ref pattern required for useCallback with empty deps. Includes the correct pattern, pre-existing stale closure in ws.onclose, and cross-references to isCancellingRef and isBuilderUpdate.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify whether agent-browser verification is required for pure bug-fix features. The fullstack-worker skill mandates TDD (RED then GREEN) and agent-browser verification. For bug-fix features where the expected behavior is fully specified and no new UI is introduced, TDD order is less meaningful. Consider adding: 'For bug-fix features (no new UI components), implementation-first then test-verify is acceptable. Agent-browser verification is still required if the fix affects UI rendering; it may be skipped for backend-only or logic fixes.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: fix-execution-results-and-cancel worker reported followedProcedure: true but transcript shows implementation-first order and skipped agent-browser step. fix-pagination-controls worker did use agent-browser correctly. The inconsistency suggests the skill guidance is ambiguous for fix features.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a note discouraging magic numbers in service-boundary code: 'Define named constants for limits and thresholds used in request handlers, particularly ones with non-obvious rationale. For example, WEBSOCKET_MAX_ROWS = 10000 with a comment explaining client-side pagination depends on receiving all rows.' The QueryResults(length=10000) in app.py is currently undocumented.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Two reviewers (fix-execution-results-and-cancel and fix-pagination-controls) independently flagged the undocumented length=10000 magic number in app.py's send_query_update().

rejectedObservations.observation: UnboundLocalError in responses.py:set_error_details for exotic ParserError subclasses
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented — architecture.md already notes this pre-existing issue. No new documentation needed.

rejectedObservations.observation: isCancellingRef not reset within cancelQuery() itself (only in resetExecution/submitQuery)
rejectedObservations.reason: non-actionable — reviewer confirmed this is harmless because wsRef is nulled in cancelQuery(), preventing any reuse of the old isCancellingRef state. Not a convention issue, just a style observation.

rejectedObservations.observation: WebSocket test does not explicitly verify length=10000 parameter
rejectedObservations.reason: non-blocking suggestion from fix-pagination-controls reviewer. Not a systemic issue or missed requirement; the feature passes its specified expected behavior. A follow-up improvement, not a blocking finding.
